research.title: Clinical Research Project Assistant
description: Examine association between high level moderation to physical activities \& social networking and less fatigue \& depression among youth with multiple sclerosis

Independently develop and maintain native iOS application, ATOMIC, to connect youth with Multiple Sclerosis to information and support for living a healthy active life as part of an international research program
Develop a React web data dashboard, which allows coaching staff and researchers to manipulate, visualize and better understand real-time data and behaviours resulting from our clinical trial
Analyze, design, prototype, develop, test in the software development lifecycle as well as provide end-user support for the ATOMIC iOS and web applications

research.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
description: Investigated changes in galaxies’ centre mass densities during galaxy quenching and blackholes’ masses correlations
Processed IllustrisTNG cosmological magnetohydrodynamical simulations data to statistically visualize compaction effect at the end of galaxies evolution on high-performance computing clusters

experience.title: Stable Matching Quantum Algorithm
description: Utilized Grover’s algorithm and designed a quantum algorithm ($O(n\sqrt{n})$) which outperformed the classical Gale-Shapley stable matching algorithm ($O(n^2)$) in terms of time complexity
Researched and designed search blackboxes for all entities in the proof of concept demo
Designed method to compute stability of all possible stable matches and return the most stable matching pair with an accuracy of 75\% on Qiskit Aer quantum computing simulator

experience.title: Variable Star Photometry
description: Compiled a calibrated dataset from raw data with Python by reducing the effects of atmospheric extinction and cosmic rays with Python and several astrophysics packages (e.g. SEP)
Applied numerous operations on dataset with and derived meaningful information using domain knowledge in astrophysics (e.g. luminosity periodicity in variable stars)
Produced an observation proposal regarding the ideal target and telescope usage time calculation and a journal paper with methodology, calculation results and graphical results

experience.title: Anomaly Detection in Power Grid Data
description: Explored unlabeled data and gave seasonality of power grid usage trend by visualizing the data and using linear regression with R
Identified a valid time window and used Max-min method in detecting point anomaly
Used log-likelihood method for contextual anomaly detection by constructing and training a Hidden Markov Model with given training data

technique: Python (+ machine learning \& astronomy libraries and quantum computing frameworks)
